params.name: Orlov Diamond
params.description: The Orlov Diamond is one of the world's famous large cut diamonds, named after Count Orlov of Russia. The diamond's rough stone was initially discovered in Golconda, India, and later set in a statue in an Indian temple. A French deserter stole the diamond, and after several changes of hands, it was eventually acquired by Count Orlov, who named it after himself. Count Orlov presented the diamond to Catherine the Great in hopes of winning back her favor. However, the Empress accepted the diamond b …
